title: Five-thirty coffee
summary: An Australian software engineer in Ho Chi Minh City slowly learns why his neighbours drink coffee before the sun is even up.
body:
My name is Sofia. I am from Melbourne, Australia. I am a software engineer. My company sent me to Ho Chi Minh City for two years.

In my first week, I noticed something strange. At five-thirty in the morning, the small street outside my building was already loud. People were drinking coffee. They sat on small plastic stools.

In Melbourne, I drank coffee at nine. At my desk. Alone.

Here, the coffee was at five-thirty. Outside. With other people.

For two weeks, I did not understand. Then I asked my neighbour. She is sixty. Her name is Mrs Lan. She works at a market.

'No, dear,' she said. 'My market opens at seven. I do not need to come out at five-thirty. I come because it is the best part of the day.'

'The best part?'

'Yes. The air is cool. The motorbikes are not loud yet. Everybody is awake but nobody is working. We talk. We drink. We are people, not workers.'

Now I have lived here ten months. I get up at five. I go down to the small coffee shop on the corner. I sit on a small plastic stool. I drink black coffee with ice.

But the old men nod at me. Mrs Lan asks about my mother in Australia. The coffee is bitter and good.

My friends in Melbourne ask, 'Is it not too early?'

I tell them, 'It is the best part of the day. The rest is just work.'

annotations.meaningVi: để ý thấy / nhận ra (quá khứ của notice)

grammarNotes.title: Time phrases: 'at' for clock times, 'in' for parts of the day
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'at + giờ' cho mốc giờ cụ thể: 'at five-thirty', 'at nine', 'at seven'. Dùng 'in + the morning/afternoon/evening' cho buổi (không nói giờ cụ thể): 'in the morning'. Buổi tối với mốc giờ thì có thể nói 'at nine in the evening' = lúc chín giờ tối.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'at + clock time' for specific times: 'at five-thirty', 'at nine', 'at seven'. Use 'in + the morning/afternoon/evening' for parts of the day (no clock): 'in the morning'. You can combine: 'at nine in the evening' = at nine in the evening.

translationVi:
Tên tôi là Sofia. Tôi đến từ Melbourne, Úc. Tôi là kỹ sư phần mềm. Công ty gửi tôi sang Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh hai năm.

Tuần đầu tiên, tôi để ý một chuyện lạ. Năm giờ rưỡi sáng, con đường nhỏ trước nhà đã ồn. Người ta đang uống cà phê. Họ ngồi trên những cái ghế nhựa thấp.

Ở Melbourne, tôi uống cà phê lúc chín giờ. Ở bàn làm việc. Một mình.

Ở đây, cà phê uống lúc năm giờ rưỡi. Ngoài đường. Cùng người khác.

Hai tuần liền, tôi không hiểu. Rồi tôi hỏi bà hàng xóm. Bà sáu mươi tuổi. Tên là cô Lan. Bà bán ở chợ.

'Không phải đâu, cháu,' bà nói. 'Chợ của bà bảy giờ mới mở. Bà không cần ra ngoài lúc năm rưỡi. Bà ra vì đó là khúc hay nhất trong ngày.'

'Khúc hay nhất ạ?'

'Ừ. Không khí mát. Xe máy chưa ồn. Ai cũng đã thức nhưng chưa ai đi làm. Mình nói chuyện. Mình uống. Mình là con người, không phải người làm.'

Giờ tôi đã sống ở đây mười tháng. Tôi dậy lúc năm giờ. Tôi xuống quán cà phê ở góc phố. Tôi ngồi trên cái ghế nhựa nhỏ. Tôi uống cà phê đen đá.

Nhưng các ông già gật đầu chào tôi. Cô Lan hỏi thăm mẹ tôi ở Úc. Cà phê đắng và ngon.

Bạn bè tôi ở Melbourne hỏi: 'Sớm quá không?'

Tôi nói: 'Đó là khúc hay nhất trong ngày. Phần còn lại chỉ là việc thôi.'

questions.prompt: What surprised her in her first week?
options: The street was already loud at 5:30 a.m. with people drinking coffee
There was no coffee for sale
questions.explanation: 'At five-thirty in the morning, the small street outside my building was already loud.'

questions.prompt: When did Sofia drink coffee in Melbourne?
options: At nine, at her desk, alone
questions.explanation: 'In Melbourne, I drank coffee at nine. At my desk. Alone.'

questions.prompt: Why does Mrs Lan come out at 5:30, if her market only opens at 7?
options: Because it is the best part of the day — cool, quiet, people are awake but not yet working
Because the market actually opens at 6
questions.explanation: 'I come because it is the best part of the day.' She then lists reasons.

questions.prompt: What is special about 5:30 a.m. according to Mrs Lan?
options: The air is cool, the motorbikes are not loud, everyone is awake but not yet working
It is the start of school

questions.prompt: What does 'We are people, not workers' mean?
options: Before the workday starts, they relate to each other as humans, not as job roles
The market does not need workers

questions.prompt: How does Sofia answer her Melbourne friends?
options: 'It is the best part of the day. The rest is just work.'
'I am tired all the time now.'
'I want to come back to Melbourne.'
'It is too early for me too.'
A morning routine that looks 'too early' from outside can be the most human part of the day to those who live it
Ho Chi Minh City has the best coffee in the world
5:30 is the only correct time to wake up
questions.explanation: The piece is built around this realisation.

blanks.explanation: The old men nod at her.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mrs Lan says, 'We are people, not workers.' Is there an hour in your day when this is true for you — when nothing is being produced and you are simply with others? If there is none, what would have to change in your week to create one?
